read.image: reeling through the midnight streets, and never more alone
read.take: She is grieving her childhood while she is barely out of it, and that is the trick of the whole thing. The setup reads like freedom — a drink spilled, "Lover's Spit" left on repeat, her mum and dad letting her stay home. But under every line sits the same low hum: it drives you crazy, getting old. The night out that should feel like a party turns into reeling through the midnight streets with people all around her, and she has never felt more alone. Then the floor opens. I want 'em back, she chants, the minds we had, the thoughts that moved around our heads, mourning a self she has only just lost. So she reaches for the oldest comfort she knows, a friend in the bed beside her like a sleepover, laughing till their ribs get tough. And she already knows the answer before she finishes asking. But that will never be enough.
read.contradiction: She climbs into a child's bed with the only friend she needs and knows in the same breath that the closeness can't keep either of them from getting old.

arc.label: The Stolen Night
arc.scene: Her parents let her stay home and the night is hers. A spilled drink, a song on repeat, the feel of being young and unwatched. But the dread is already in the room. It drives you crazy, getting old.

arc.label: The Dream Sours
arc.scene: The same night turns. She's reeling through the midnight streets with a crowd around her and can't feel a single one of them. This dream isn't feeling sweet anymore. It feels so scary, getting old.

arc.label: The Mourning
arc.scene: The dread cracks into full grief. I want 'em back, she repeats, the minds we had, the thoughts that moved around our heads. She is at a funeral for a childhood self who isn't even cold yet, and it's not enough to feel the lack.

arc.label: The Clinging
arc.scene: She reaches for the one thing left. A friend in the bed beside her like little kids, laughing till their ribs get tough. The comfort is real and she holds it close. But that will never be enough, over and over, until the night runs out.

lines.line: It drives you crazy, getting old
And I've never felt more alone

I want 'em back, I want 'em back
The minds we had, the minds we had
But that will never be enough

read.texture: A hushed, half-acoustic pulse opens it, sparse enough to hear the room, then the voice stacks on itself and swirls into an echo that keeps swelling. The tempo sits up near a dance beat, but the mood underneath is almost without color, so the swell never lifts you. It just circles, a party that keeps spinning and never warms.
